ogTitle: Hollands Landing vs Homerton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hollands Landing and Homerton.
heading: Hollands Landing vs Homerton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hollands Landing, Victoria and Homerton, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hollands Landing has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Homerton (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hollands Landing or Homerton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hollands Landing has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Homerton scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hollands Landing scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Homerton scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hollands Landing or Homerton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hollands Landing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
